title: Model Formulation, Identifiability, and Validation · Quantity Clinic: Identifiability
summary: Build, test, and communicate a model involving energy demand and storage. Planned artifact: Add and verify the math adv identifiability increment in a reproducible model rigor computational study. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Formulate identifiability in a dimensioned applied model and defend the method's assumptions.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""applied-mathematics-advanced cumulative model portfolio."""

from math import exp, log, sqrt


def reasonableness(value: float, lower: float, upper: float) -> bool:
    return lower <= value <= upper

steps.title: Calculate Identifiability evidence
steps.instruction: A measured quantity starts at 21 units, changes by −8 units, then by +6 units. Calculate the final quantity.
steps.why: A numerical result is not complete until its unit, assumptions, scale, and tolerance survive a reasonableness check.

content.text: Preserve the signs and compute 21 − 8 + 6; compare with the starting scale.

content.title: Before calculating
content.text: Predict the sign and approximate magnitude. Enter the requested quantity with a unit, then compare it with that prediction.

hints: Preserve the signs and compute 21 − 8 + 6; compare with the starting scale.
Keep full working precision and round only the final reported value.
The accepted result is within 0.001 of the exact value and includes units.

steps.title: Defend the Identifiability model
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Identifiability applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Formulate identifiability in a dimensioned applied model and defend the method's assumptions.
